top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The world's mood today is jittery. A drone slipped past UAE defences and started a fire at the Barakah nuclear plant — no radiation leak, but a stark reminder that the US-Iran ceasefire is fraying. Ukraine landed one of its biggest-ever drone strikes on Moscow, killing four, while Israel and Lebanon extended their ceasefire even as Israeli air strikes kept killing civilians. Markets opened the week nervous, with oil up sharply and US stock futures down.

stories.headline: Drone strike sparks fire at UAE's Barakah nuclear plant
stories.summary: A drone got past Emirati air defences on Sunday and hit an electrical generator just outside the perimeter of the Barakah nuclear power plant near Abu Dhabi. No radiation leaked and reactor units kept running, but the UAE called it a major security breach. The strike came as US-Iran tensions over the Strait of Hormuz boil over again.

stories.headline: Ukraine launches one of its largest drone strikes ever on Moscow region
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones flew more than 500 km overnight to hit Moscow, killing at least four people including a woman in Khimki and two men in a village north of the capital. About 12 more were wounded near a Moscow oil refinery. Russian air defences shot down 81 drones in one of the boldest Ukrainian raids of the war.

stories.headline: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended 45 days, but strikes kill 5 in south Lebanon
stories.summary: Washington announced a 45-day extension of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire after talks at the State Department. Within hours, Israeli air strikes killed at least five people in southern Lebanese towns including two children. A formal security track is meant to start May 29, with the next round of talks in early June.

stories.headline: Trump-Xi summit wraps up with tariff truce extension expected
stories.summary: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping wrapped two days of meetings in Beijing focused on tariffs, rare earths, agricultural buying and Taiwan. Traders priced an 81% chance the existing tariff truce gets extended — keeping the average US tariff on Chinese goods at 47.5% rather than letting it snap higher. Xi warned Trump that mishandling Taiwan would put the relationship in 'great jeopardy'.

stories.headline: Oil jumps and stock futures fall as Iran tensions resurface
stories.summary: Brent crude rose near $110 and WTI near $106 a barrel on Monday as the UAE drone strike and renewed US-Iran sabre-rattling spooked traders. US futures slid after Friday's 1%-plus sell-off across the major indexes, with Nvidia earnings later this week the next big test for sentiment.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: A quieter Sunday for Singapore but a busy week ahead. Senior Minister Lee Hsien Loong heads to China for a five-day visit starting today, while shoppers and commuters get ready for late-starting Thomson-East Coast Line train services and afternoon thundery showers. Health authorities say two residents who were on the hantavirus-hit MV Hondius tested negative and remain in precautionary quarantine — the public-risk level stays low.

stories.headline: SM Lee Hsien Loong begins five-day visit to China
stories.summary: Senior Minister Lee Hsien Loong is in China from 18 to 22 May, visiting Guangxi and Shanghai. The trip lands during a busy Asia diplomatic week — coming right after the Trump-Xi Beijing summit — and is being read as Singapore reaffirming its long-standing balancing act with its largest trading partner.

stories.headline: Thundery showers on most afternoons for the rest of May
stories.summary: NEA's fortnight outlook says short thundery showers are expected over parts of the island on most afternoons through the end of May, occasionally extending into the evening. Sumatra squalls could also bring widespread heavier rain with gusty winds during the pre-dawn and morning hours on a few days. Bring an umbrella for the commute home.

stories.headline: Two Singapore residents on hantavirus-hit cruise ship test negative
stories.summary: The Communicable Diseases Agency confirmed that the two men (aged 65 and 67) isolated at NCID after possible exposure to the MV Hondius outbreak tested negative for hantavirus, including the Andes virus. They stay in 30-day quarantine as a precaution, followed by phone surveillance until the 45-day incubation window closes. CDA says risk to the public remains low.

stories.headline: Thomson-East Coast Line shuttle buses for late-start Saturdays from 23 May
stories.summary: Three shuttle bus services along key TEL MRT stations will run on Saturday mornings from 23 May to 4 July while train services start later than usual. LTA is asking weekend commuters to plan ahead — the shuttles cover the gap before TEL trains begin operating.

stories.headline: Economic Strategy Review delivers 32 recommendations across 8 thrusts
stories.summary: Last week, the Economic Strategy Review released its final eight 'thrusts' covering 32 recommendations, with the newest focused on energy and supply-chain resilience after recent geopolitical shocks like the Strait of Hormuz crisis. Expect government follow-ups on these themes in coming months.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: All three theatres got hotter today, not cooler. Ukraine showed it can reach Moscow at will; Russia answered with a heavy overnight barrage on Ukraine. In the Gulf, a drone hit a UAE nuclear plant and Tehran kept threatening to re-close the Strait of Hormuz. And in Lebanon, a freshly extended ceasefire is already being broken by Israeli air strikes that killed five civilians in the south.

theatres.name: Russia-Ukraine War
theatres.status: Escalating — major mutual strikes; US push for a June peace deadline ongoing

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine.
Trump-brokered talks intensify; Zelensky says a 20-point peace framework is '90% agreed' but Donbas territory remains a sticking point.
Russia and Ukraine declare separate, partial ceasefires around the WWII anniversary; they quickly fray.
Putin publicly hints he may end the war, but Kremlin still rejects current US proposal as 'absolutely unacceptable' on several points.
Major Ukrainian long-range strikes hit Moscow region overnight; Zelensky hails 'significant' shift in momentum.
One of the largest Ukrainian drone attacks of the war: 4 killed (3 near Moscow, 1 in Belgorod), 12 wounded near a Moscow oil refinery; 81 drones shot down by Russian defences.
Russia retaliates with combined drone-and-missile attack on central and southern Ukraine: 524 drones and 22 missiles launched; 20 wounded including 2 children; 503 drones and 4 missiles intercepted.

theatres.name: US/Israel-Iran Conflict (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Fragile ceasefire under heavy strain; nuclear plant struck in UAE

timeline.event: US and Israel launch coordinated airstrikes that kill Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei — war begins.
Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz 'closed', triggering global oil-price shock and Asian fuel shortages.
Conditional US-Iran ceasefire pauses hostilities; Israel-Lebanon track also opens.
US launches counter-blockade of the Strait, targeting any ship attempting to reach Iranian ports.
Tehran says it won't 'control' the Strait but Washington keeps the blockade; brinkmanship continues.
Iran says it will soon unveil a Hormuz transit-toll mechanism; Pakistan continues to mediate.
Drone strike hits a generator at the UAE's Barakah nuclear power plant. UAE blames Iran-aligned actors. No radiation leak; reactor units operating normally.

theatres.name: Israel-Lebanon / Gaza
theatres.status: Ceasefire extended 45 days but Israeli strikes continue; Hezbollah opposes direct talks

timeline.event: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire begins after months of cross-border war — but is repeatedly violated from the start.
Israel keeps demolishing homes in southern Lebanon and occupying border villages despite the truce.
US State Department announces a 45-day ceasefire extension after Washington talks; a US-facilitated security track is to begin 29 May.
Israeli airstrikes hit Tayr Felsay, Tayr Debba, Az-Zrariyah and Jebchit — at least 5 killed including 2 children. Hezbollah legislator calls direct negotiations a 'dead-end path'.

hantavirus.label: Hantavirus Outbreak
hantavirus.ai_summary: The MV Hondius outbreak is spreading slowly but quietly across borders. As of mid-May there are 11 cases (9 confirmed, 2 suspected) and 3 deaths, with Canada the newest country to flag a presumptive case. Singapore's two suspected residents tested negative. WHO still calls the wider epidemic risk low — Andes virus only spreads through close, sustained contact — but case fatality runs 20-40%, so health agencies in 12+ countries are still tracking former passengers.

stories.headline: WHO situation report: 11 cases, 3 deaths so far in MV Hondius cluster
stories.summary: As of 12 May 2026, WHO counts 9 confirmed and 2 suspected hantavirus infections traced to the Dutch cruise ship MV Hondius, with 3 deaths (2 confirmed Andes virus). WHO has shipped 2,500 diagnostic kits from Argentina to labs in five countries and deployed an expert onboard. The agency rates the epidemic risk as low.

stories.headline: Canada reports presumptive positive case from cruise passenger
stories.summary: Canada notified the WHO on 16 May that a former MV Hondius passenger tested presumptively positive for Andes hantavirus, becoming the latest country to identify a case linked to the ship. Confirmatory testing is pending.

stories.headline: Both Singapore residents test negative; remain in 30-day quarantine
stories.summary: Singapore's CDA confirmed that the two residents isolated at NCID (aged 65 and 67) tested negative for hantavirus and the Andes virus. They share a flight history with a confirmed case who died in South Africa. They will stay quarantined for 30 days from last exposure, then 15 more days of phone monitoring.

stories.headline: Former passengers tracked across 12+ countries on five continents
stories.summary: Former MV Hondius passengers are now hospitalised or quarantined across Australia, Canada, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Saint Helena, Singapore, South Africa, Spain, Switzerland, Turkey and the US. The 147 people onboard (86 passengers, 61 crew) came from 23 countries, complicating contact tracing.

stories.headline: Experts: pandemic risk low, but case-fatality rate is 20-40%
stories.summary: Public-health experts at Johns Hopkins and elsewhere stress that Andes virus does not spread efficiently and an epidemic of COVID-style scale is unlikely. The worry is the disease's lethality — historic outbreaks have killed 20-40% of confirmed cases — so identifying every exposed person early is critical.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: Big week for offensive security. Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 wrapped with researchers earning a record $1.29M for 47 new zero-days against Windows 11, Microsoft Edge, SharePoint, VMware ESXi and AI tools like LiteLLM. On the defensive side, CISA's deadline to patch a critical Cisco SD-WAN auth-bypass (CVE-2026-20182) hit on 17 May, Microsoft's May Patch Tuesday fixed 120 flaws including a wormable DNS-client RCE, and Grafana disclosed that GitHub tokens were stolen from its repos.

stories.headline: Cisco SD-WAN auth bypass (CVE-2026-20182) — federal patch deadline hit on May 17
stories.summary: A critical authentication-bypass flaw in Cisco's Catalyst SD-WAN Controller is being used in zero-day attacks to gain admin access on compromised devices. CISA added it to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog and ordered all federal agencies to patch by Sunday 17 May. Private-sector defenders should treat it as urgent.

stories.headline: Microsoft May Patch Tuesday fixes 120 flaws including a wormable DNS RCE
stories.summary: Microsoft's May 2026 Patch Tuesday addresses 120 vulnerabilities, 17 of them rated Critical. Most notable: CVE-2026-41096, a Windows DNS Client RCE where a malicious DNS server can send a specially-crafted reply that runs code on a target machine. No zero-days disclosed in this cycle.

stories.headline: Pwn2Own Berlin 2026: $1.29M paid for 47 zero-days; DEVCORE wins Master of Pwn
stories.summary: Three days of competition produced 47 brand-new zero-day exploits against Windows 11, Microsoft Edge, SharePoint, VMware ESXi, Red Hat Enterprise Linux and AI tools including LiteLLM. STARLabs' Nguyen Hoang Thach earned $200K for a cross-tenant code-execution exploit against VMware ESXi. DEVCORE took the Master of Pwn title.

stories.headline: Grafana discloses GitHub token theft by unauthorized party
stories.summary: Grafana Labs disclosed on May 17 that an unauthorized party obtained GitHub access tokens belonging to the company. Customers and contributors should review recent activity and rotate any potentially exposed secrets. Investigation is ongoing.

stories.headline: Instructure pays ShinyHunters ransom to halt 3.65TB Canvas data leak
stories.summary: Canvas parent Instructure reached an 'agreement' with the ShinyHunters extortion group after attackers stole 275 million Canvas records spanning thousands of schools and universities. The decision to pay reduces immediate leak risk but underscores how reliant the education sector has become on a single learning-management vendor.

stories.headline: CISA flags VMware Aria Operations RCE flaw as exploited in attacks
stories.summary: CISA added a VMware Aria Operations remote-code-execution flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog this week, warning that attackers are using it in the wild. Federal civilian agencies must patch within the standard KEV deadline.
